title: Getting Started
code: two_way_freq_table <- countmaskr_data %>%   count(race, gender) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = gender, values_from = n) %>%   mutate(     across(all_of(c("Female", "Male", "Other")), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), 0, .)),     Overall = Female + Male + Other,     .after = 1   )  mask_table(   two_way_freq_table,   col_groups = list(c("Overall", "Female", "Male", "Other")),   overwrite_columns = TRUE,   percentages = FALSE ) %>%   kable()

title: Small Cell Masking Tool for One- & Two-Way Tabular Reports
text: dissemination aggregate health statistics derived clinical administrative data carries inherent tension analytic utility patient confidentiality. reported frequencies sufficiently small, individuals within subgroup may vulnerable re-identification — particularly stratified cross-tabulated outputs demographic, geographic, clinical covariates intersect. vulnerabilities prompted formal regulatory responses, culminating data use agreement (DUA) obligations codified federal agencies clinical research networks alike. Federal agencies — including CMS, AHRQ, NCI, CDC — maintain formal small-cell suppression requirements condition data access publication. Clinical research networks similarly enforce standards: PCORnet® PEDSnet require minimum cell-size threshold 11 across distributed data queries respective data sharing agreements. studies spanning multiple reporting dimensions — stratified demographic breakdowns multi-site analyses — manually identifying suppressing qualifying primary complementary cells across large tables cumbersome error-prone process. countmaskr automates workflow end--end, enabling analysts meet DUA obligations consistently reproducible manner across institutional data sharing pipelines.

title: Small Cell Masking Tool for One- & Two-Way Tabular Reports
text: small cell  : cell value defined threshold requires suppression. Aka, primary cell secondary cell  : cell within contingency table suppression prevent reverse engineering small(primary) cell arithmetic operations

title: countmaskr: Small Cell Masking Tool for One- & Two-Way Tabular Reports — countmaskr-package
text: Provides automated small-cell suppression one- two-way frequency tables. Cells falling user-defined frequency threshold masked, suppression propagated secondary cells prevent indirect disclosure. Designed clinical health administrative data, package supports range tabular structures fits reproducible reporting pipelines, reducing manual review applying consistent suppression rules across data sharing workflows.

what: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 1 - A1) — mask_counts
title: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 1 - A1) — mask_counts
text: Identifies primary secondary cells numeric vector masks according specified threshold.

title: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 1 - A1) — mask_counts
Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 1 - A1) — mask_counts
text: x numeric vector. threshold positive numeric value specifying threshold values must suppressed. Default 11. zero_masking Logical; TRUE, zeros can masked secondary cells present. Default FALSE. secondary_cell Character string specifying method selecting secondary cells necessary. Options "min", "max", "random". Default "min".

title: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 1 - A1) — mask_counts
text: character vector primary /secondary masked cells.

title: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 1 - A1) — mask_counts
text: function operates two main steps: primary masking secondary masking. Primary Masking: Values greater 0 less specified threshold considered primary cells. values masked replacing <threshold. Secondary Masking: Secondary masking applied prevent deduction masked primary cells totals. logic identifying need secondary masking based following conditions: Condition : one primary masked cell exists, counts greater equal threshold. Condition B: Two counts 1 masked, counts greater equal threshold. Condition C: threshold 11, two counts 10 masked, counts greater equal threshold. conditions met, secondary masking performed follows: zero_masking TRUE zeros present data, one zero randomly selected masked <threshold. zeros masked present, non-zero cell selected masking based secondary_cell parameter: "min": smallest unmasked count greater zero selected. "max": largest unmasked count selected. "random": random unmasked count selected. selected secondary cell masked calculating new masking threshold using formula: $$mask\_value = 5 \times \lceil (selected\_value + 1) / 5 \rceil$$ formula calculates masking threshold first adding 1 selected value, dividing 5, rounding nearest whole number. result multiplied 5 get final mask_value. Essentially, rounds selected value next multiple 5 incrementing 1. cell replaced <mask_value.

title: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 1 - A1) — mask_counts
code: x1 <- c(5, 11, 43, 55, 65, 121, 1213, 0, NA) x2 <- c(1, 1, 1, 55, 65, 121, 1213, 0, NA) x3 <- c(11, 10, 10, 55, 65, 121, 1213, 0, NA)  mask_counts(x1) #> [1] "<11"   "<15"   "43"    "55"    "65"    "121"   "1,213" "0"     NA      mask_counts(x2) #> [1] "<11"   "<11"   "<11"   "<60"   "65"    "121"   "1,213" "0"     NA      mask_counts(x3) #> [1] "<15"   "<11"   "<11"   "55"    "65"    "121"   "1,213" "0"     NA       if (requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE) && requireNamespace("tidyr", quietly = TRUE)) {   data("countmaskr_data")    aggregate_table <- countmaskr_data %>%     dplyr::select(-c(id, age)) %>%     tidyr::gather(block, Characteristics) %>%     dplyr::group_by(block, Characteristics) %>%     dplyr::summarise(N = dplyr::n()) %>%     dplyr::ungroup()    aggregate_table %>%     dplyr::group_by(block) %>%     dplyr::mutate(N_masked = mask_counts(N)) } #> Warning: attributes are not identical across measure variables; they will be dropped #> `summarise()` has regrouped the output. #> ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by block and Characteristics. #> ℹ Output is grouped by block. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(block, Characteristics))` for per-operation grouping #>   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead. #> # A tibble: 16 × 4 #> # Groups:   block [4] #>    block     Characteristics                       N N_masked #>    <chr>     <chr>                             <int> <chr>    #>  1 age_group 18-29                               243 243      #>  2 age_group 30-39                               198 198      #>  3 age_group 40-49                               215 215      #>  4 age_group 50-64                               323 323      #>  5 age_group 65+                                 521 521      #>  6 ethnicity Hispanic                            143 143      #>  7 ethnicity Non-Hispanic                       1346 1,346    #>  8 ethnicity Other                                11 11       #>  9 gender    Female                              728 <730     #> 10 gender    Male                                763 763      #> 11 gender    Other                                 9 <11      #> 12 race      American Indian/ Pacific Islander    66 <70      #> 13 race      Asian                               215 215      #> 14 race      Black                               453 453      #> 15 race      Other                                 6 <11      #> 16 race      White                               760 760

what: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 2 - A2) — mask_counts_2
title: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 2 - A2) — mask_counts_2
text: function masks values numeric vector based specified threshold, using primary secondary masking ensure data privacy.

title: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 2 - A2) — mask_counts_2
Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 2 - A2) — mask_counts_2
text: x Numeric vector mask. threshold Positive numeric value threshold cells masked. Default 11. zero_masking Logical; TRUE, zeros may masked secondary cells present. Default FALSE.

title: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 2 - A2) — mask_counts_2
text: character vector masked cells, retaining NA NA_character_.

title: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 2 - A2) — mask_counts_2
text: function operates two main steps: Primary Masking: Values greater 0 less threshold masked replacing <threshold. Secondary Masking: Applied additional masking required prevent deduction masked cells totals. Secondary masking triggered following conditions: Condition : single primary masked cell exists, values meet exceed threshold. Condition B: Two counts 1 masked, values meeting exceeding threshold. Condition C: threshold set 11, two counts 10 masked counts meeting exceeding threshold. conditions met: zero_masking = TRUE zeros present, one zero randomly selected masked <threshold. zero_masking = FALSE (zeros absent), function masks largest unmasked count (.e., maximum non-zero value). Formula Mask Value Calculation: calculate mask_value secondary cell, following formula used: $$mask\_value = selected\_value - (threshold - totals\_of\_small\_cells)$$ words, formula subtracts difference threshold sum small cells (masked primary masking step) selected maximum unmasked value. adjusted mask_value helps ensure privacy retaining consistent totals.

title: Perform threshold-based cell masking with primary and secondary masking (Algorithm 2 - A2) — mask_counts_2
code: x1 <- c(5, 11, 43, 55, 65, 121, 1213, 0, NA)  mask_counts_2(x1) #> [1] "<11"    "11"     "43"     "55"     "65"     "121"    ">1,207" "0"      #> [9] NA        if (requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE) && requireNamespace("tidyr", quietly = TRUE)) {   data("countmaskr_data")   countmaskr_data %>%     dplyr::select(-c(id, age)) %>%     tidyr::gather(block, Characteristics) %>%     dplyr::group_by(block, Characteristics) %>%     dplyr::summarise(N = dplyr::n()) %>%     dplyr::ungroup() %>%     dplyr::mutate(N_masked = mask_counts_2(N)) } #> Warning: attributes are not identical across measure variables; they will be dropped #> `summarise()` has regrouped the output. #> ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by block and Characteristics. #> ℹ Output is grouped by block. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(block, Characteristics))` for per-operation grouping #>   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead. #> # A tibble: 16 × 4 #>    block     Characteristics                       N N_masked #>    <chr>     <chr>                             <int> <chr>    #>  1 age_group 18-29                               243 243      #>  2 age_group 30-39                               198 198      #>  3 age_group 40-49                               215 215      #>  4 age_group 50-64                               323 323      #>  5 age_group 65+                                 521 521      #>  6 ethnicity Hispanic                            143 143      #>  7 ethnicity Non-Hispanic                       1346 1,346    #>  8 ethnicity Other                                11 11       #>  9 gender    Female                              728 728      #> 10 gender    Male                                763 763      #> 11 gender    Other                                 9 <11      #> 12 race      American Indian/ Pacific Islander    66 66       #> 13 race      Asian                               215 215      #> 14 race      Black                               453 453      #> 15 race      Other                                 6 <11      #> 16 race      White                               760 760

what: Apply Threshold-Based Masking to a Data Frame — mask_table
title: Apply Threshold-Based Masking to a Data Frame — mask_table
text: mask_table function applies threshold-based masking specified columns data frame. uses mask_counts function mask counts certain threshold, adhering data privacy requirements. function can handle grouped data calculate percentages required. ensures convergence checking specific criteria iteration.

title: Apply Threshold-Based Masking to a Data Frame — mask_table
code: mask_table(   data,   threshold = 11,   col_groups,   group_by = NULL,   overwrite_columns = TRUE,   percentages = FALSE,   perc_decimal = 0,   zero_masking = FALSE,   secondary_cell = "min",   .verbose = FALSE )

title: Apply Threshold-Based Masking to a Data Frame — mask_table
text: data data frame containing counts masked. Must data frame. threshold positive numeric value specifying threshold values must suppressed. Default 11. col_groups character vector list character vectors, character vector specifies columns data masking applied. group_by optional character string specifying column name data group data masking. overwrite_columns Logical; TRUE, original columns overwritten masked counts. FALSE, new columns added masked counts. Default TRUE. percentages Logical; TRUE, percentages calculated masked accordingly. Default FALSE. perc_decimal = positive numeric value specifying decimals percentages. Default 0. zero_masking Logical; TRUE, zeros can masked secondary cells present. Passed mask_counts. Default FALSE. secondary_cell Character string specifying method selecting secondary cells necessary. Options "min", "max", "random". Passed mask_counts. Default "min". .verbose Logical; TRUE, progress messages printed masking. Default FALSE.

title: Apply Threshold-Based Masking to a Data Frame — mask_table
text: data frame masked counts specified columns. percentages = TRUE, additional columns percentages added. structure returned data frame depends overwrite_columns parameter.

title: Apply Threshold-Based Masking to a Data Frame — mask_table
code: data("countmaskr_data")  aggregate_table <- countmaskr_data %>%   select(-c(id, age)) %>%   gather(block, Characteristics) %>%   group_by(block, Characteristics) %>%   summarise(N = n()) %>%   ungroup() #> Warning: attributes are not identical across measure variables; they will be dropped #> `summarise()` has regrouped the output. #> ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by block and Characteristics. #> ℹ Output is grouped by block. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(block, Characteristics))` for per-operation grouping #>   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.  mask_table(aggregate_table,   group_by = "block",   col_groups = list("N") ) #> # A tibble: 16 × 3 #>    block     Characteristics                   N     #>    <chr>     <chr>                             <chr> #>  1 age_group 18-29                             243   #>  2 age_group 30-39                             198   #>  3 age_group 40-49                             215   #>  4 age_group 50-64                             323   #>  5 age_group 65+                               521   #>  6 ethnicity Hispanic                          143   #>  7 ethnicity Non-Hispanic                      1,346 #>  8 ethnicity Other                             11    #>  9 gender    Female                            <730  #> 10 gender    Male                              763   #> 11 gender    Other                             <11   #> 12 race      American Indian/ Pacific Islander <70   #> 13 race      Asian                             215   #> 14 race      Black                             453   #> 15 race      Other                             <11   #> 16 race      White                             760    mask_table(aggregate_table,   group_by = "block",   col_groups = list("N"),   overwrite_columns = FALSE,   percentages = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 16 × 6 #>    block     Characteristics                     N N_masked N_perc N_perc_masked #>    <chr>     <chr>                           <int> <chr>    <chr>  <chr>         #>  1 age_group 18-29                             243 243      16 %   16 %          #>  2 age_group 30-39                             198 198      13 %   13 %          #>  3 age_group 40-49                             215 215      14 %   14 %          #>  4 age_group 50-64                             323 323      22 %   22 %          #>  5 age_group 65+                               521 521      35 %   35 %          #>  6 ethnicity Hispanic                          143 143      10 %   10 %          #>  7 ethnicity Non-Hispanic                     1346 1,346    90 %   90 %          #>  8 ethnicity Other                              11 11       1 %    1 %           #>  9 gender    Female                            728 <730     49 %   <49 %         #> 10 gender    Male                              763 763      51 %   51 %          #> 11 gender    Other                               9 <11      1 %    masked cell   #> 12 race      American Indian/ Pacific Islan…    66 <70      4 %    <5 %          #> 13 race      Asian                             215 215      14 %   14 %          #> 14 race      Black                             453 453      30 %   30 %          #> 15 race      Other                               6 <11      0 %    masked cell   #> 16 race      White                             760 760      51 %   51 %           countmaskr_data %>%   count(race, gender) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = gender, values_from = n) %>%   mutate(across(all_of(c("Male", "Other")), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), 0, .)),     Overall = Female + Male + Other, .after = 1   ) %>%   countmaskr::mask_table(.,     col_groups = list(c("Overall", "Female", "Male", "Other")),     overwrite_columns = TRUE,     percentages = FALSE   ) #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   race                              Overall Female Male  Other #>   <chr>                             <chr>   <chr>  <chr> <chr> #> 1 American Indian/ Pacific Islander 66      <30    <40   0     #> 2 Asian                             215     <100   118   <11   #> 3 Black                             453     <225   228   <11   #> 4 Other                             NA      NA     <11   0     #> 5 White                             760     379    <375  <11

what: Perturb Counts in a Vector with Small Cells — perturb_counts
title: Perturb Counts in a Vector with Small Cells — perturb_counts
text: perturb_counts function perturbs counts numeric vector containing small cells, specifically one primary cell present secondary cells need masked, following Algorithm 3 (A3). function adjusts counts distributing noise non-primary cells preserving overall distribution much possible.

title: Perturb Counts in a Vector with Small Cells — perturb_counts
Perturb Counts in a Vector with Small Cells — perturb_counts
text: x Numeric vector length N containing counts. threshold Numeric value specifying threshold small cells (primary cells). Defaults 10.

title: Perturb Counts in a Vector with Small Cells — perturb_counts
text: character vector perturbed counts formatted digit precision thousands separator. perturbation feasible, function returns counts masked using mask_counts().

title: Perturb Counts in a Vector with Small Cells — perturb_counts
text: Perturbation Process Overview: function performs perturbation following steps: Identification Small Cells: Cells counts greater 0 less specified threshold identified small cells (primary cells). $$\text{Small Cells} = \{ \mid 0 < x_i < \text{threshold} \}$$ Adjustment Small Cells: counts small cells set threshold value. $$x'_i = \left\{      \begin{array}{ll}        \text{threshold} & \text{} x_i \text{ small cell} \\        x_i & \text{otherwise}      \end{array}    \right.$$ Calculation Total Noise: total noise distributed calculated difference original total sum adjusted sum. $$\text{Total Noise} = \sum_{=1}^{N} x_i - \sum_{=1}^{N} x'_i$$ Distribution Noise Non-Small Cells: total noise proportionally distributed non-small cells based original counts. Weights Calculation: $$w_i = \frac{x_i}{\sum_{j \\text{Non-Small Cells}} x_j}$$ Noise Allocation: $$\text{Noise}_i = w_i \times \text{Total Noise}$$ Adjusted Counts: $$x''_i = x'_i + \text{Noise}_i$$ Rounding Adjusted Counts: adjusted counts rounded nearest integer. $$x'''_i = \text{round}(x''_i)$$ Adjustment Rounding Discrepancies: remaining noise due rounding discrepancies adjusted iteratively adding subtracting 1 largest counts total counts balanced, ensuring count falls threshold. Verification Proportions: function checks proportions non-small cells remain consistent perturbation. proportions differ, function coerces mask counts using mask_counts() function. Coercion Mask Counts: function coerces mask counts following scenarios: Multiple Small Cells Detected: one small cell identified, perturbation may necessary unless intended use. function still proceed perturbation recommends using threshold-based suppression. Insufficient Available Counts: non-small cells enough counts absorb total noise without count falling threshold, operation lead information loss. Proportions Changed Perturbation: perturbation alters original proportions non-small cells, operation lead information loss. #' - Counts Threshold: counts vector specified threshold, meaningful perturbation possible. case, function coerces mask_counts() secure alternative. cases, function calls mask_counts() apply threshold-based cell suppression secure alternative.

title: Perturb Counts in a Vector with Small Cells — perturb_counts
code: # Example vectors x1 <- c(5, 11, 43, 55, 65, 121, 1213, 0, NA) x2 <- c(1, 1, 1, 55, 65, 121, 1213, 0, NA) x3 <- c(11, 10, 10, 55, 65, 121, 1213, 0, NA)  # Apply the function lapply(list(x1, x2, x3), perturb_counts) #> Warning: Total primary cells: 3. Threshold-based suppression is recommended. See mask_counts() & mask_counts_2() #> [[1]] #> [1] "10"    "11"    "43"    "55"    "65"    "121"   "1,208" "0"     NA      #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "10"    "10"    "10"    "54"    "64"    "119"   "1,190" "0"     NA      #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] "11"    "10"    "10"    "55"    "65"    "121"   "1,213" "0"     NA      #>   # Using the function within a data frame data("countmaskr_data") aggregate_table <- countmaskr_data %>%   select(-c(id, age)) %>%   tidyr::gather(block, Characteristics) %>%   group_by(block, Characteristics) %>%   summarise(N = n()) %>%   ungroup() #> Warning: attributes are not identical across measure variables; they will be dropped #> `summarise()` has regrouped the output. #> ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by block and Characteristics. #> ℹ Output is grouped by block. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(block, Characteristics))` for per-operation grouping #>   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.  aggregate_table %>%   group_by(block) %>%   mutate(N_masked = perturb_counts(N)) #> # A tibble: 16 × 4 #> # Groups:   block [4] #>    block     Characteristics                       N N_masked #>    <chr>     <chr>                             <int> <chr>    #>  1 age_group 18-29                               243 243      #>  2 age_group 30-39                               198 198      #>  3 age_group 40-49                               215 215      #>  4 age_group 50-64                               323 323      #>  5 age_group 65+                                 521 521      #>  6 ethnicity Hispanic                            143 143      #>  7 ethnicity Non-Hispanic                       1346 1,346    #>  8 ethnicity Other                                11 11       #>  9 gender    Female                              728 728      #> 10 gender    Male                                763 762      #> 11 gender    Other                                 9 10       #> 12 race      American Indian/ Pacific Islander    66 66       #> 13 race      Asian                               215 214      #> 14 race      Black                               453 452      #> 15 race      Other                                 6 10       #> 16 race      White                               760 758
